title: Understanding a Science Fiction Extract and Using Parenthesis
explanation_chunks: This page introduces you to an exciting science fiction story where a boy named Gavin is saved by a robot called Eager from some 'marauders' (which are like thieves).
We learn new words from the story like 'enfolding' (meaning trapping) and 'pinioned' (meaning pinned). It's important to keep a log of new words to improve your vocabulary!
You'll practice summarising the story, which means telling the main parts in your own words, but in a much shorter way. Try to keep it to about 50 words.

We'll also look at 'parenthetic commas' or dashes. These are like little pauses that add extra information into a sentence without starting a new one. They help to make your writing more detailed and interesting.
Finally, you'll get to plan and write your own new part of the story, imagining more adventures for Gavin and Eager! Remember to use exciting verbs and descriptive words.
examples: Original sentence: 'The taller boy pushed Gavin back to the ground, snatched the sports bag and ran off, while his accomplice threw the jinn into the road and followed him.'
Parenthetic example: 'Fleur – Gavin's sister – didn't like Eager at first.' (The dashes add extra info about Fleur).
Story planning: Think about a chase, a battle, or a clever meeting for your new story section.
key_points: Read science fiction stories to expand your imagination.
Improve your vocabulary by logging new words and using mnemonics (memory tricks).
Summarise texts to understand the main points concisely.
Use parenthetic commas or dashes to add extra information into sentences, making your writing richer.
Plan your creative writing carefully before you start to write, using vivid verbs.

vocabulary.definition: thieves or raiders who steal things
a helper, usually for a bad deed
a fictional piece of technology in this story
pinned or held tightly, usually arms or wings

explain_again: This page is about reading an exciting robot story and learning new words. We also learn how to add little extra bits of information into our sentences using special commas or dashes, like a secret whisper to the reader. Then, we get to plan our own adventure for the characters!
fun_analogy: Parenthetic commas or dashes are like secret whispers or side comments you make while telling a story. They add extra juicy details without stopping the main conversation!
